item.title: ST 552: Statistical Methods II
item.description:
Description: Simple and multiple linear regression including polynomial regression, indicator variables, weighted regression, and influence statistics, nonlinear regression and linear models for binary data.

  • Winter 2021 (remote format)
  • Winter 2022

item.title: ST 553: Statistical Methods III
item.description:
Description: Principles and analysis of designed experiments, including factorial experiments, analysis of covariance, random and mixed effect models.

  • Spring 2018
  • Spring 2020 (remote format)

item.title: ST 518: Data Analytics II
item.description:
Description: Statistical methods and data analysis techniques for count data. Topics include tests for tables of counts, logistic regression, log-linear regression, generalized linear mixed models, and issues for large datasets. Data analysis in R.

  • Spring 2017

item.title: ST 431/531: Sampling Methods
item.description:
Description: Estimation of means, totals and proportions; sampling designs including simple random, stratified, cluster, systematic, multistage and double sampling; ratio and regression estimators; sources of errors in surveys; capture-recapture methods.

  • Fall 2016
  • Fall 2017
  • Fall 2018
  • Fall 2019
  • Fall 2020 (remote format)
  • Fall 2021

item.title: ST 411/511: Methods of Data Analysis
item.description:
Description: Graphical, parametric and nonparametric methods for comparing two samples; one-way and two-way analysis of variance; simple linear regression.

  • Winter 2018
  • Winter 2019

item.title: ST 407/507/509: Consulting Practicum
item.description:
Description: The student provides statistical advice, under faculty guidance, on university-related research projects.

More information: https://stat.oregonstate.edu/content/student-consulting-services

This course is co-taught with 50% effort.

  • Fall 2017
  • Fall 2018
  • Winter 2019
  • Spring 2019
  • Fall 2019
  • Winter 2020
  • Spring 2020 (remote format)

item.title: Targeted Random Door-To-Door Sampling Design for COVID-19 Informed by Community Wastewater
The following posts were helpful to me as I was developing this website
item.description:
General Setup

  • http://www.statslab.cam.ac.uk/~qz280/post/migrating/
  • https://discourse.gohugo.io/t/howto-install-hugo-on-mac/768

Troubleshooting Error Messages

  • http://user.it.uu.se/~justin/Hugo/post/hugo_module_fail/
  • https://zenn.dev/meihei/articles/32bb275f71e938

References

  • https://iphysresearch.github.io/blog/post/writting-markdown/

item.title: This is my dog, Zeus!
item.description:
Zeus

Zeus was born in (approximately) June 2016. He is a German Shepherd/Chihuahua mix (along with some other small dog breeds) and weighs around 34 pounds. He enjoys hiking, eating cheese, playing with squeaky toys, and taking naps.

item.title: A Bayesian framework for modelling the preferential selection process in respondent-driven sampling
Pinot noir crop estimation method allows growers to estimate yields earlier than lag phase
Populations size estimations using SS-PSE among men who have sex with men in four European cities: How many MSM are living with HIV?
Phosphorus supply does not affect Fusarium crown rot of winter wheat
The U.S. academic fisheries co-authorship network under the lens of diversity and inclusion
Linking subsistence harvest diversity and productivity to adaptive capacity in an Alaskan food sharing network
Reaction of winter wheat and barley cultivars to Fusarium pseudograminearum-innoculated fields in the dryland Pacific Northwest, USA
Population dynamics of wheat root pathogens under different tillage systems in NE Oregon
Population size estimations among hidden populations using respondent-driven sampling surveys: Case studies from Armenia
Empirical assessment of programs to promote collaboration: A network model approach
Measuring a hidden population: A novel technique to estimate the population size of women with sexual violence related pregnancies in South Kivu Province, Democratic Republic of Congo
Example Project
item.description:

item.title: A novel method for estimating the size of hidden populations using respondent-driven sampling data: Case examples from Morocco
Vineyard floor management and cluster thinning inconsistently affect ‘Pinot noir’ crop load, berry composition, and wine quality
Workload Estimates for Risk-Limiting Audits of Large Contests
Assaying organochlorines in archived serum for a large, long-term cohort: Implications of combining assay results from multiple laboratories over time
